name: Active Scanning
description: Adversaries may execute active reconnaissance scans to gather information that can be used during targeting.
detection: Monitor for suspicious network traffic patterns indicating scanning activity.

name: Gather Victim Network Information
description: Adversaries may gather information about the victim's networks that can be used during targeting.
detection: Monitor for lookups of domain registration data, DNS records, and network topology.

name: Gather Victim Identity Information
description: Adversaries may gather information about the victim's identity that can be used during targeting (emails, credentials, names).
detection: Monitor for credential stuffing attempts and data broker queries.

name: Search Open Technical Databases
description: Adversaries may search freely available technical databases for information about victims (Shodan, Censys, CT logs).
detection: Difficult to detect — passive reconnaissance against third-party databases.

name: Gather Victim Host Information
description: Adversaries may gather information about the victim's hosts (OS, hardware, software, patches).
detection: Monitor for banner grabbing and service probing activity.

name: Network Service Discovery
description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts and local network infrastructure devices.
detection: Monitor for unusual port scanning activity, sequential connection attempts.

name: System Information Discovery
description: Adversaries may attempt to get detailed information about the operating system and hardware.
detection: Monitor for execution of system information commands (systeminfo, uname, cat /etc/os-release).

name: Account Discovery
description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of accounts on a system or within an environment.
detection: Monitor for commands like net user, Get-ADUser, cat /etc/passwd.

name: Network Share Discovery
description: Adversaries may look for folders and drives shared on remote systems to identify sources of information.
detection: Monitor for net share, smbclient, and similar enumeration commands.

name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
description: Adversaries may attempt to exploit a weakness in an Internet-facing host or program.
detection: Monitor application logs for unusual requests, WAF alerts, error spikes.

name: Brute Force
description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown.
detection: Monitor for multiple failed login attempts, account lockouts.

name: OS Credential Dumping
description: Adversaries may attempt to dump credentials to obtain account login and credential material (hashes, tickets).
detection: Monitor for LSASS access, SAM database reads, DCSync traffic.

name: Valid Accounts
description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts as a means of gaining access.
detection: Monitor for unusual login times, locations, and impossible travel.

name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.
detection: Monitor for unusual PowerShell, Bash, Python execution patterns.

name: Scheduled Task/Job
description: Adversaries may abuse task scheduling functionality to facilitate initial or recurring execution of malicious code.
detection: Monitor for new scheduled tasks, cron jobs, at jobs.

name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
description: Adversaries may circumvent mechanisms designed to control elevated privileges (UAC bypass, sudo abuse, SUID).
detection: Monitor for UAC bypass attempts, unusual sudo usage, SUID binary execution.

name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
description: Adversaries may exploit software vulnerabilities to escalate privileges.
detection: Monitor for kernel exploit indicators, unusual process crashes, privilege changes.

name: Remote Services
description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into remote services (RDP, SSH, SMB, WinRM).
detection: Monitor for unusual remote login patterns, new source IPs.

name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets
description: Adversaries may attempt to subvert Kerberos authentication (Kerberoasting, Golden/Silver tickets).
detection: Monitor for unusual TGS requests, RC4 encryption in Kerberos traffic.

name: Traffic Signaling
description: Adversaries may use traffic signaling to hide open ports or trigger special actions on a system.
detection: Monitor for unusual packet patterns preceding port openings.
